title: Calibration accepts incomplete camera params and breaks downstream consumers
summary: A reproducible calibration issue lets incomplete camera parameters pass into laser-plane fitting. The failure then appears later in downstream matching and reconstruction instead of stopping at the calibration boundary.
symptoms: Non-finite plane coefficients appear after calibration
expected_behavior: Calibration should reject incomplete camera parameters before fitting the laser surface.
actual_behavior: The workflow continues with invalid calibration data and only fails after downstream modules consume it.
reproduction_steps: Load a calibration payload with missing intrinsic matrix values.
Observe downstream mismatch or reconstruction failure instead of an early validation error.
suspected_impact: The fault originates in calibration but can regress stripe_matching and reconstruction_3d because all downstream consumers trust CalibrationResult.
